channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: STAT+: A ‘historic’ FDA clearance raises the question: Is LLM the interface? Or the decision-maker?
item.description: The newly cleared device is based around an app that helps a patient manage their diabetes using a treatment plan defined by their doctor.

item.title: Targeted enzyme discovery using metal-coordination mining
item.description: A methodology for mining protein structure databases on the basis of the distinct intrinsic structures of metal-binding active sites in enzymes enables the discovery of new families of radical halogenases.

item.title: Towards the construction of a virtual yeast
item.description: A virtual yeast, an artificial intelligence-driven agent for modelling eukaryotic cellular behaviours, is described.

item.title: Kpmg ran an internal exam to certify staff on the ethical use of artificial intelligence — 28 employees were caught using artificial intelligence to cheat on it, including a partner who was fined $10,000
item.description: KPMG Australia confirmed in February 2026 that 28 of its staff had used artificial intelligence tools to cheat on internal training exams since July 2025, after the firm introduced monitoring systems specifically designed to detect unauthorised AI use during …

item.title: U2af1 mutations rescue deleterious exon skipping induced by kras mutations
item.description: KRASG12S mutations generate potential docking sites for splicing factor U2AF1, which can lead to exon skipping and nonfunctional KRAS transcripts. This is mitigated by mutations in U2AF1, which rescue exon skipping, thereby restoring KRAS activity in lung ade…

item.title: What cios need to know about AI notetaking security
item.description: AI meeting tools enhance collaboration and efficiency but introduce serious privacy, compliance and data governance risks.

item.title: AI agents are making marketing decisions on data no one has checked in years
item.description: Ask anyone in ad tech about data governance, and they’ll talk about the supply side. The industry spent years building verification frameworks for publishers and sellers, proving that the data powering supply-side decisions is what it claims to be. Standards …

item.title: Review highlights strong safety and effectiveness of mrna vaccines
item.description: A comprehensive new review published in The Lancet affirms that currently approved mRNA vaccines are safe and highly effective at preventing infectious diseases, including COVID-19.

item.title: Biological aging might help to explain the rising risk of early-onset cancer
item.description: Cancers have risen rapidly in younger adults in recent generations. Analyzing large population-based cohorts, we found that recent generations exhibited signs of faster biological aging than older ones, and that systemic and organ-specific aging markers were …

item.title: Trop2 targeting reveals therapy-driven cell state dynamics in colorectal cancer
item.description: TROP2 marks poor-prognosis colorectal cancer cells with fetal-like, therapy-resistant states, and therapeutic targeting of TROP2 with antibody–drug conjugates in combination with chemotherapy enhances treatment efficacy by modulating tumour cell plasticity an…

item.title: Improved heritability partitioning and enrichment analyses using summary statistics with graphreml
item.description: graphREML is a likelihood-based heritability partitioning method that uses genome-wide association studies summary statistics and linkage disequilibrium graphical models. It yields enrichment estimates concordant with S-LDSC but shows greater power and produc…

item.title: Electronic health and care records specialist group bi-monthly webinar
item.description: Join our bi-monthly EHCR Community of Practice meetings to share innovation, advance shared care records, and tackle health inequalities. Join us on Thursday, 21 January 2027 from 12:00pm.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on anthropic's mythos and fable models | techcrunch
item.description: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined fable 5 is greenlit to return
item.description: After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access tomorrow. Anthropic: We've received notice that the Departme…

item.title: Gabapentin Extended Release Tablets in Healthy Subjects Under Fed Conditions: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Treatment, Two-Period, Two-Sequence, Single Dose, Crossover, Comparative Bioavailability Study
item.description: This randomized, open-label, crossover bioavailability study compared gabapentin extended-release tablets in healthy subjects under fed conditions using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence design with single-dose administration. The study was conducted by Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited

item.title: Cytotoxicity and synergistic anticancer activity of Kigelia africana (Lam.) Benth. and Spathodea campanulata P. Beauv. polyherbal extracts
item.description: This study evaluated the cytotoxic and anticancer properties of polyherbal extracts combining Kigelia africana and Spathodea campanulata, investigating their individual and synergistic effects against cancer cell lines. The research demonstrates that these plant extracts exhibit cytotoxic activity a

item.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Young Children with Sickle Cell Disease
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a supplemental approval for Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) for patients aged 2 years and older with either sickle cell disease (SCD) with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs) or transfusion-dependent β thalassemia (TDT).

item.title: Statins and blood pressure drugs changing health risks of obesity, study suggests
item.description: New research shows obesity patients over 40 are managing blood pressure and cholesterol as well as healthy-weight peers, but younger adults remain at risk.

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Anthropic drug development goals, Trump drug plan snags, and more
item.description: Anthropic announced the availability of Claude Science, which optimizes its large language model for use in scientific labs and by drugmakers

item.title: STAT+: More signs that GLP-1s may help with peripheral artery disease
item.description: Anthropic will begin developing drugs, BridgeBio pockets a billion, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: FDA says Zyn can market its pouches as safer than cigarettes
item.description: The influence of the alcohol industry on policy, AI Claude for scientists, and more health news from Morning Rounds

item.title: STAT+: FDA approves Orca Bio’s T cell therapy for blood cancer patients
item.description: The FDA approved a T-cell therapy from Orca Bio that reduces the risk of a debilitating immune reaction in patients with blood cancers undergoing stem cell transplants.

item.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
item.description: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

item.title: The Download: Anthropic launches Claude Science, and California’s carbon manure math
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers yesterday, Anthropi

item.title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
item.description: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.

item.title: Rnabpflow: base pair-augmented se(3) flow matching for conditional rna 3d structure generation
item.description: RNAbpFlow generates all-atom RNA conformational ensembles for single-chain RNA monomers without the explicit or implicit use of evolutionary information or homologous structural templates.

item.title: AI will drive africa’s sustainable development, conference declares
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been identified as a critical catalyst for accelerating innovation, strengthening governance, improving institutional resilience and advancing sustainable development across Africa and the Global South, as experts at an intern…

item.title: Hpe discover 2026: powering siemens healthineers’ life-saving vision
item.description: Siemens Healthineers’ head of clinical marketing discusses the technology behind the firm’s advances in medical imaging

item.title: Retraction note: nsd2 targeting reverses plasticity and drug resistance in prostate cancer
item.description: Nature - Retraction Note: NSD2 targeting reverses plasticity and drug resistance in prostate cancer

item.title: In an era of AI darwinism, roi now represents return on intelligence
item.description: You can’t cut your way to growth. You can’t automate your way to innovation. AI is forcing every organization to reveal what it really believes about people. Do leaders see AI as a way to shrink the company into a more efficient version of yesterday? Or do th…

item.title: Vibe coding platform base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility | techcrunch
item.description: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

item.title: Ophthalmic self-medication among adult ophthalmic patients attending a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia: a mixed-methods study assessing prevalence, associated factors and lived experience
item.description: This mixed-methods study evaluated the prevalence of self-medication practices for eye conditions among adult patients at a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia, identifying associated risk factors and documenting patients' firsthand experiences with self-treatment. The research combined q

item.title: Safety evaluation of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies: a pharmacovigilance study using FDA adverse event reporting system
item.description: This pharmacovigilance study analyzed adverse event reports from the FDA system to evaluate the safety profile of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies, a class of immunotherapeutic agents designed to engage both T cells and B cells in cancer treatment. The research systematically identified and characteri

item.title: Collaborative development of a digital intervention to support opioid tapering after surgery in primary care: an experience-based co-design study with patients and clinicians
item.description: Researchers used experience-based co-design methodology involving patients and clinicians to develop a digital intervention aimed at supporting opioid tapering in primary care following surgical procedures. The collaborative approach integrated patient and healthcare provider perspectives to create

item.title: FDA Approves New Treatment That Uses Donor Immune Cells to Prevent Serious Complications in Blood Cancer Patients
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved TREGZI, the first regulatory T (Treg) cell-based immunotherapy for improving chronic graft-versus-host disease (GVHD)-free survival in adult patients with blood cancers undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT).

item.title: STAT+: Anthropic releases Claude Science, a product aimed at researchers, the pharma industry
item.description: Anthropic released Claude Science, an application that optimizes its large language model for scientists and, especially, those doing research at pharma companies.

item.title: STAT+: Investors double down on Bain-backed startup
item.description: FDA's top gene therapy regulator steps down, Trump's plan to lower GLP-1 prices, and more biotech news

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

item.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
item.description: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.

item.title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Engineers on the new team will embed within companies to deploy purpose-built agents, focusing on fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

item.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
item.description: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously c

item.title: The Download: AI “coworkers” and stratospheric internet
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI agents are not your “coworkers” Imagine coming in to work to learn that a new underling will report to you. The worker is not a person but an AI to

item.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
item.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork.  The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins

item.title: Building tech in the world’s secret R&D hub
item.description: Apple. Anthropic. Disney Research. Google. Meta. Microsoft. NVIDIA. OpenAI. Few places outside Silicon Valley can claim R&D hubs from all of these companies. Fewer still are concentrated in a city of just over 400,000 people—roughly half the size of San Francisco. Over the past two decades, howe

item.title: Anthropic's new AI workbench mapped my field for $26. now imagine it aimed at the rest of science
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Science is a capable AI research workbench built for molecular biology. The opportunities for the rest of science are enormous.

item.title: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program, joining tech giants in betting on healthcare
item.description: Anthropic is starting an internal drug discovery program as part of an effort to develop artificial intelligence tools designed for drugmakers, becoming the latest company to try and crack the healthcare market. At an event in San Francisco on Tuesday, Anthro…

item.title: Jon and mindy gray bet $55m on AI to catch cancer before it starts
item.description: A new institute at Penn’s Basser Center will use artificial intelligence and biomarkers to intercept hereditary cancers at their earliest stages, before they become disease. The idea behind the gift is unusual enough to need its own word. Most cancer philanth…

item.title: Q&a: generate biomedicines CTO talks AI advancements
item.description: Generate Biomedicines has been AI-native since its founding in 2018. Hear from co-founder and CTO Gevorg Grigoryan about how the biotech company uses AI to its advantage.
